prose:
The fukui-reactivity task was performed on nitrobenzene using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier `standard`), in the gas phase. The calculation used charge 0 and multiplicity 1 for the neutral molecule. The input geometry was read from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz`.

The most electrophilic atom (where f+ is highest) is Carbon (atom 4, 0-indexed: 3) with a Fukui f+ index of 0.49641782.
The most nucleophilic atom (where f- is highest) is Carbon (atom 5, 0-indexed: 4) with a Fukui f- index of 0.1072882.
